response.justification: Wei Chen is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with 19+ years of experience, but the Principal Performance Architect role requires specialized expertise in hardware architecture, SoC/IP modeling, and GPU accelerator optimization—none of which are demonstrated in their background. Their career trajectory has been consistently software-focused (web, mobile, backend, AI-agent infrastructure), with no evidence of work in silicon-level performance modeling, hardware co-design, or accelerator architecture that the principal position demands.
strengths: 19+ years of proven software engineering expertise with demonstrated ability to optimize performance-critical systems (low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
Recent exposure to AI infrastructure through current Staff role at re:cinq, showing awareness of modern AI workload challenges at the software layer
Systems programming background with C/C++ and DLang experience, plus proven ability to learn diverse technical domains across web, mobile, embedded, and backend
concerns: No demonstrated experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware design, or architectural decisions at the silicon level—the core technical focus of the role
Complete absence of GPU architecture, AI accelerator optimization, SIMD/MIMD architecture, or deep learning workload performance analysis experience required for principal-level position
Entire career trajectory is software-centric with no progression toward hardware performance engineering, hardware manufacturing considerations, or hardware-software co-design
